Context.purpose: Ship v1 as a thin MCP proxy over tg-chat-rag with an internal admin surface for API key lifecycle management.
Context.scope: Expose search_messages, get_message_context, get_related_messages, and list_sources on /mcp; provide /admin/login and /admin/api-keys UI with key create/revoke controls.

constraints.constraint-1: External MCP callers cannot provide filters.chat_ids.
constraints.constraint-2: search_messages always uses chat IDs from TG_CHAT_RAG_CHAT_IDS env.
constraints.constraint-3: All upstream tg-chat-rag calls use Bearer token from TG_CHAT_RAG_BEARER_TOKEN.
constraints.constraint-4: /mcp accepts only Bearer API keys from the API key repository.
constraints.constraint-5: ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN is valid only for /admin/login and must never authorize /mcp.
constraints.constraint-6: API key raw secret is shown exactly once at creation time; only key hash is stored.

contract.purpose: Load and validate runtime configuration for MCP proxy, admin authentication, and PostgreSQL connectivity.

export-loadConfig.PURPOSE: Validate env and return typed runtime config

type-AppConfig.PURPOSE: Typed app config with TG_CHAT_RAG settings, ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN, and DATABASE_URL

contract.purpose: Provide structured logs for MCP requests, admin actions, validation decisions, upstream calls, and failures.
Create and expose Drizzle PostgreSQL database client from DATABASE_URL runtime configuration.

export-createDb.PURPOSE: Initialize Drizzle client and pooled PostgreSQL connection

type-DbClient.PURPOSE: Typed Drizzle database handle used by repositories

contract.purpose: Persist and query API key lifecycle records used for MCP Bearer authentication.

export-createApiKey.PURPOSE: Generate raw key material, persist hashed key, and return one-time secret

export-listApiKeys.PURPOSE: List non-revoked and revoked API keys with label and expiry metadata

export-resolveApiKey.PURPOSE: Resolve presented Bearer token by hash and expiry/revocation status

notes.note-1: Table contract: id, key_hash (unique), key_prefix, label, expires_at (nullable), revoked_at (nullable), created_at.
notes.note-2: Raw API key is never stored; only cryptographic hash and non-sensitive prefix are persisted.

contract.purpose: Define and export MCP tool schemas and tool allowlist for the proxy surface.

export-SearchMessagesInputPublic.PURPOSE: Public schema for search_messages with filters.chat_ids forbidden

export-PROXIED_TOOL_NAMES.PURPOSE: Constant allowlist for proxied tool names

notes.note-1: search_messages schema is intentionally not 1:1 with upstream: filters.chat_ids is forbidden at public boundary.

contract.purpose: Call tg-chat-rag methods API via POST /api/v1/methods/<tool_name> with Bearer auth.

export-callMethod.PURPOSE: Execute proxied tool call to tg-chat-rag methods API

contract.purpose: Authenticate admin operators with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN and enforce cookie session checks for /admin routes.

export-authenticateAdmin.PURPOSE: Verify login token and produce admin session cookie

export-requireAdminSession.PURPOSE: Check admin session and decide allow/redirect

export-clearAdminSession.PURPOSE: Clear admin auth cookie on logout

notes.note-1: Default unauthenticated admin behavior: 302 redirect to /admin/login.
notes.note-2: ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN is accepted only by admin login flow and never by MCP bearer auth.

contract.purpose: Authorize /mcp requests using API keys only and reject unauthorized callers before transport handling.

export-authorizeMcpRequest.PURPOSE: Validate Bearer key against API key repository and return allow/deny

notes.note-1: No fallback credentials are allowed for /mcp; ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN is rejected by design.

contract.purpose: Apply policy-safe normalization and proxy supported MCP tool calls to tg-chat-rag.

export-executeTool.PURPOSE: Validate args, inject internal chat_ids policy, call upstream, normalize result

notes.note-1: Defense-in-depth: reject requests containing filters.chat_ids at public input level.
notes.note-2: For search_messages, always set filters.chat_ids from TG_CHAT_RAG_CHAT_IDS after validation.

contract.purpose: Render admin HTML with Bun JSX and process HTMX actions for API key management.

export-renderAdminLayout.PURPOSE: Render sidebar layout with Api Keys tab

export-renderApiKeysTable.PURPOSE: Render HTMX-refreshable API keys list fragment

notes.note-1: UI stack: Bun JSX templating + HTMX progressive enhancement + daisyUI class primitives.
notes.note-2: Sidebar contains one tab: Api Keys.

contract.purpose: Register MCP tools and route authorized tool calls into ToolProxyService.

export-registerTools.PURPOSE: Register proxied MCP tools except list_chats

notes.note-1: Registered tools: search_messages, get_message_context, get_related_messages, list_sources.

M-SERVER.NAME: ServerMain

contract.purpose: Boot Bun HTTP server, enforce auth guards, and expose /mcp, /admin/*, and /healthz routes.

export-main.PURPOSE: Boot sequence, dependency wiring, and route lifecycle

M-SERVER.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-DB, M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY, M-ADMIN-AUTH, M-ADMIN-UI, M-MCP-AUTH-GUARD, M-TRANSPORT

DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.NAME: Admin Login Flow
DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.step-1: Operator submits login form with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN candidate.
DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.step-2: M-ADMIN-AUTH validates token and issues signed HTTP-only admin session cookie.

DF-ADMIN-GUARD.NAME: Admin Route Guard Flow
DF-ADMIN-GUARD.step-4: Authenticated request is routed to M-ADMIN-UI handlers.

DF-API-KEY-CREATE.NAME: API Key Create Flow
DF-API-KEY-CREATE.step-1: Operator submits label and optional expires_at.
DF-API-KEY-CREATE.step-2: M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY generates raw secret, stores only hash+metadata in PostgreSQL, returns one-time secret payload.
DF-API-KEY-CREATE.step-3: M-ADMIN-UI returns updated key list fragment and one-time secret panel.

DF-API-KEY-REVOKE.NAME: API Key Revoke Flow
DF-API-KEY-REVOKE.step-3: M-ADMIN-UI returns refreshed API key list fragment.

DF-MCP-AUTH.NAME: MCP Bearer Auth Flow
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-1: Client sends Authorization header with Bearer token.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-2: M-MCP-AUTH-GUARD resolves token hash and checks expiry/revocation.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-3: Unauthorized request is rejected before MCP transport invocation.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-4: Authorized request is dispatched to M-TRANSPORT.

DF-PROXY-SEARCH.NAME: search_messages Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool search_messages.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-2: M-TRANSPORT validates against public schema that forbids filters.chat_ids.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-4: M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT sends POST {TG_CHAT_RAG_BASE_URL}/api/v1/methods/search_messages with Bearer token.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-5: Response is normalized and returned to MCP client.

DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.NAME: get_message_context Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool get_message_context.
DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-PROXY-RELATED.NAME: get_related_messages Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-RELATED.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool get_related_messages.
DF-PROXY-RELATED.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-PROXY-SOURCES.NAME: list_sources Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-SOURCES.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool list_sources.
DF-PROXY-SOURCES.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.NAME: list_chats Exclusion Flow
DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.step-2: M-TRANSPORT has no registration for list_chats.
DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.step-3: Client receives MCP tool-not-found behavior; no upstream call is performed.

Phase-1.name: Existing Proxy Foundation
Phase-1.step-1: Implement typed env parsing and strict validation for TG_CHAT_RAG_* variables.
Phase-1.step-2: Implement structured logger and request-scoped fields.
Phase-1.step-3: Implement public schemas and proxied tool allowlist constants.

Phase-2.name: Existing Proxy Core
Phase-2.step-1: Implement Bearer-auth upstream client and error normalization.
Phase-2.step-2: Implement validation, chat_ids policy injection, forwarding, and response mapping.

Phase-3.name: Existing Entrypoints
Phase-3.step-1: Register 4 tools and wire tool calls to proxy service.
Phase-3.step-2: Start Bun HTTP server for /mcp and /healthz with graceful shutdown.
Phase-3.step-3: Add smoke checks: 4 allowed tools pass, list_chats is not exposed.

Phase-4.name: Admin Data Foundation
Phase-4.step-1: Extend config contract with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN and DATABASE_URL validation.
Phase-4.step-3: Create API key schema and repository methods for create/list/revoke/resolve.

Phase-5.name: Admin Auth and UI
Phase-5.step-1: Implement /admin/login authentication and cookie session checks.
Phase-5.step-2: Implement admin layout, sidebar, and HTMX API keys CRUD interactions.

Phase-6.name: MCP Authorization Integration
Phase-6.step-1: Implement Bearer API key guard with expiry/revocation checks.
Phase-6.step-2: Wire /mcp guard and /admin routes into server fetch handler.
Phase-6.step-3: Add integration smoke tests for unauthorized/authorized /mcp requests.

RISK-1.name: Upstream Schema Drift
RISK-1.impact: Proxy payload contracts can diverge from tg-chat-rag while that service is still evolving.
RISK-1.mitigation: Keep schemas centralized in M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS and run smoke contract checks for all proxied tools.

RISK-2.name: Upstream Availability
RISK-2.impact: Timeouts or 5xx from tg-chat-rag can degrade MCP reliability.
RISK-2.mitigation: Use explicit request timeout, stable error mapping, and structured logs with tool_name and upstream status.

RISK-3.name: Chat Scope Leakage
RISK-3.impact: If callers could pass chat_ids, they might bypass intended source scope.
RISK-3.mitigation: Public schema forbids filters.chat_ids and proxy layer enforces reject-if-present plus internal injection.

RISK-4.name: One-Time Secret Handling
RISK-4.impact: Operator may lose newly generated API key if copied incorrectly.
RISK-4.mitigation: Explicit one-time reveal panel with operator warning and clear regeneration path via create new key.

RISK-5.name: Session Cookie Security
RISK-5.impact: Weak admin session implementation can expose admin controls.
RISK-5.mitigation: Use HTTP-only + SameSite cookie, signature validation, and strict redirect behavior for unauthenticated admin routes.

RISK-6.name: Key Material Exposure
RISK-6.impact: Persisting raw keys or verbose logs could leak credentials.
RISK-6.mitigation: Persist only hash+prefix, redact auth headers in logs, and never render full key except immediately after creation.

RISK-7.name: Clock Drift and Expiry Validation
RISK-7.impact: Expiry checks may be inconsistent across environments if time handling is naive.
RISK-7.mitigation: Use database/server UTC timestamps and explicit expiry comparison semantics.

RISK-8.name: Database Availability for Auth Path
RISK-8.impact: /mcp authorization depends on PostgreSQL; outages block all MCP calls.
RISK-8.mitigation: Fail closed on auth checks, surface clear health diagnostics, and alert on DB connectivity failures.
